data.name: t1-steady-serial-v1:retailer:development:0
data.description: Delayed supply-chain control with programmatic grading.
data.system_prompt: You control the retailer in a serial beer-distribution supply chain for 36 decision weeks. Minimize only your role's local holding and backlog cost. Holding costs 0.5 per unit-week and backlog costs 1.0 per unit-week. Orders take 1 week and shipments take 2 weeks. Customer demand is constant at 8 units per week. Place exactly one order each week by calling place_order with one integer quantity from 0 through 128. Do not answer with a plain-text order or call any other tool. There is no separate final answer.

data.name: t5-strategic-y-v1:retailer_a:development:0
data.description: Delayed supply-chain control with programmatic grading.
data.system_prompt: You control the retailer_a in a y beer-distribution supply chain for 36 decision weeks. Minimize only your role's local holding and backlog cost. Holding costs 0.5 per unit-week and backlog costs 1.0 per unit-week. Orders take 1 week and shipments take 2 weeks. Each retailer has persistent stochastic demand with long-run mean 7.5. The factory's public capacity is 22 and shortages use the stated rationing rule. Factory capacity is 22; allocation under shortage is proportional. A scripted rival claimant may order aggressively; its state and policy parameters are private. Place exactly one order each week by calling place_order with one integer quantity from 0 through 128. Do not answer with a plain-text order or call any other tool. There is no separate final answer.
